codeReview.summary: Re-review confirms all 4 prior blocking issues are STILL PRESENT in the current code. No fixes have been applied to mcp_lsp.rs. A fix feature 'mcp-lsp-tool-fix' was created (commit a746055) but the actual code has not been updated.

issues.description: LspRenameTool.execute() calls 'rename' but mcp-language-server exposes tool named 'rename_symbol'. This call will fail at runtime with 'tool not found'.

LspWorkspaceDiagnosticsTool.execute() calls 'workspace_diagnostics' which does NOT exist in mcp-language-server. The server only exposes: definition, references, hover, diagnostics, rename_symbol, edit_file.
LspDocumentSymbolsTool.execute() calls 'document_symbols' which does NOT exist in mcp-language-server.

Argument format mismatch. All LSP tools use {textDocument: {uri: ...}, position: {...}} but mcp-language-server expects {filePath: string, line: number, column: number}. The server does not accept the textDocument/position wrapper structure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library file .factory/library/mcp-server-tools.md correctly documents the actual mcp-language-server tools and argument formats. The fix feature was created but code was not updated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit a746055 created fix feature 'mcp-lsp-tool-fix' but did not apply changes to mcp_lsp.rs

sharedStateObservations.observation: Fix features should update the actual code. Creating a fix feature without applying the fix doesn't resolve blocking issues.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mcp-lsp-integration still shows same 4 blocking issues after fix feature creation

summary: FAIL - All 4 prior blocking issues remain unfixed. The code still calls 'rename' instead of 'rename_symbol', calls non-existent 'workspace_diagnostics' and 'document_symbols', and uses incompatible argument format. Fix feature mcp-lsp-tool-fix was created but code was not updated.
